senses.definition: A person who opposes harmony or strongly criticizes traditional ideas of harmony in music or art.
examples: Some modern composers were seen as antiharmonists by their conservative critics.
The critic, an open antiharmonist, praised the harsh and clashing sounds of the piece.

senses.definition: Preventing or reducing a wasting fever, especially in older medical use.
examples: Physicians once prescribed antihectic medicines for patients with long, slow fevers.
The herb was described in early texts as having antihectic properties.

senses.definition: Plural of antihelix: the inner curved ridge of cartilage of the outer ear, running roughly parallel to the rim.
examples: The surgeon carefully marked both antihelices before the cosmetic ear operation.
In anatomy class, students learned to identify the helix and the antihelices on the model of the ear.

senses.definition: The inner curved ridge of cartilage of the outer ear, lying just inside and roughly parallel to the outer rim.
examples: The piercing was placed along the antihelix rather than the outer rim of the ear.
Swelling around the antihelix can make wearing certain headphones uncomfortable.

senses.definition: Plural of antihelix: the inner curved ridges of cartilage of the outer ears.
examples: The antihelixes were slightly asymmetrical after the surgery.
He compared the shapes of the antihelixes in different patients.

senses.definition: Preventing or treating infections caused by parasitic worms.
examples: The doctor prescribed an antihelminthic drug for the intestinal infection.
Antihelminthic treatment is common in areas with poor sanitation.

senses.definition: A drug used to kill or remove parasitic worms from the body.
examples: The veterinarian gave the dog an antihelminthic to clear the worms.
Regular use of antihelminthics can reduce worm infections in children.

senses.definition: Drugs used to kill or remove parasitic worms from the body.
examples: Antihelminthics are widely used in mass treatment campaigns.
Some antihelminthics are effective against several types of worms.

senses.definition: An antibody that prevents or reduces the clumping together of red blood cells or other cells.
examples: The presence of antihemagglutinin can interfere with certain blood tests.
Researchers measured antihemagglutinin levels after vaccination.

senses.definition: The hemisphere on the opposite side of something, especially the brain or the Earth.
examples: The stroke affected the right hemisphere and caused changes in the antihemisphere as well.
Birds may use one hemisphere while the antihemisphere rests.

senses.definition: An antibody that reacts against hemoglobin.
examples: Antihemoglobin antibodies can affect the accuracy of some blood tests.
The study focused on the role of antihemoglobin in autoimmune reactions.

senses.definition: A substance, usually an antibody, that protects red blood cells from being broken down.
examples: Antihemolysin in the serum helped prevent damage to the red blood cells.
The test measured antihemolysin activity after exposure to the toxin.

senses.definition: Preventing or reducing the breakdown of red blood cells.
examples: The antihemolytic effect of the drug protected the patient from severe anemia.
Antihemolytic agents are important in certain blood disorders.

senses.definition: Used to treat hemophilia or helping blood to clot in people with hemophilia.
examples: He receives antihemophilic factor regularly to prevent bleeding.
Antihemophilic therapy has greatly improved patients’ quality of life.

senses.definition: Preventing or stopping heavy bleeding.
examples: The surgeon used an antihemorrhagic agent to control blood loss.
Vitamin K has antihemorrhagic properties in certain conditions.

senses.definition: Relating to the prevention or treatment of hemorrhoids.
examples: The cream contains several antihemorrheidal ingredients.
Antihemorrheidal preparations are sold over the counter in many pharmacies.

senses.definition: The main character in a story who does not have the usual good or brave qualities of a hero.
examples: The series follows an antihero who is both selfish and strangely likeable.
Many modern films feature an antihero instead of a traditional hero.

senses.definition: Plural of antihero: main characters in stories who lack the usual good or brave qualities of heroes.
examples: Recent TV dramas are full of dark, troubled antiheroes.
Writers often use antiheroes to explore moral gray areas.

senses.definition: Having qualities typical of an antihero rather than a traditional hero.
examples: Her antiheroic actions make the audience unsure whether to admire her.
The film’s antiheroic tone challenges simple ideas of good and evil.

senses.definition: A female main character in a story who does not have the usual good or brave qualities of a heroine.
examples: The novel’s antiheroine is rude and selfish but strangely charming.
Writers now create complex antiheroines in many TV shows.

senses.definition: Plural of antiheroine: female main characters who lack the usual good or brave qualities of heroines.
examples: Recent dramas feature antiheroines who make many bad choices.
Antiheroines allow writers to show women with real flaws and doubts.

senses.definition: The quality, style, or practice of using antiheroes or showing characters who lack traditional heroic qualities.
examples: The novel’s antiheroism reflects a deep distrust of traditional heroes.
Critics discussed the rise of antiheroism in modern television.

senses.definition: Working against or used to treat herpes infections.
examples: The doctor prescribed an antiherpes medication for the outbreak.
Researchers are testing new antiherpes vaccines.

senses.definition: An antibody that protects cells from being destroyed by a heterolysin (a substance that breaks down cells from another species).
examples: Antiheterolysin was detected in the patient’s serum after exposure to the animal antigen.
The experiment measured antiheterolysin levels over time.

senses.definition: Reducing or stopping sweating.
examples: An antihidrotic drug was used to treat the patient’s excessive sweating.
Antihidrotic creams may be applied to the hands and feet.

senses.definition: Opposed to fixed levels of power or status in an organization or society.
examples: The company adopted an antihierarchal management style.
Their antihierarchal beliefs led them to reject traditional authority.

senses.definition: Opposed to or lacking a clear system of higher and lower levels of power or status.
examples: The movement’s antihierarchic ideals appealed to young activists.
Their antihierarchic model gave equal voice to all members.

senses.definition: Strongly opposing or rejecting systems with clearly ranked levels of power or status.
examples: The group promotes antihierarchical forms of decision-making.
Her antihierarchical views often clash with corporate culture.

senses.definition: In a way that rejects or works against fixed levels of power or status.
examples: The project was run antihierarchically, with no formal leaders.
They tried to organize the team antihierarchically to encourage shared responsibility.

senses.definition: Plural of antihierarchy: systems or organizations that reject clear levels of power or status.
examples: Some social movements attempt to build antihierarchies instead of traditional parties.
Workplaces experimenting with antihierarchies often struggle with decision-making.

senses.definition: The belief in or support for social or organizational systems without fixed levels of power or status.
examples: Antihierarchism is a core value in some anarchist movements.
Her writing defends antihierarchism in the modern workplace.

senses.definition: A person who rejects or actively opposes systems with fixed levels of power or status.
examples: As an antihierarchist, she argued against traditional corporate structures.
The protest was organized by antihierarchists from several groups.

senses.definition: A system or way of organizing that avoids clear ranks and fixed levels of power.
examples: The group tried to create an antihierarchy, where no one had more power than others.
Running an antihierarchy can be difficult when quick decisions are needed.

senses.definition: Designed to prevent or respond to the hijacking of airplanes, vehicles, or systems.
examples: The airport installed new antihijack security measures.
Antihijack devices were fitted to all the trucks in the fleet.

senses.definition: To prevent or stop the hijacking of a vehicle, aircraft, or system.
examples: Special forces moved quickly to antihijack the plane.
The new protocol is meant to antihijack any attack on the system.

senses.definition: Actions, laws, or measures aimed at preventing or stopping hijacking.
examples: Governments have strengthened antihijacking laws in recent years.
The airline invested heavily in antihijacking technology.

senses.definition: Relating to the prevention or stopping of hijacking.
examples: They ran an antihijacking exercise at the airport.
Antihijacking units were placed on alert.

senses.definition: A drug that reduces the effects of histamine and is used to treat allergies and some stomach problems.
examples: She took an antihistamine to relieve her hay fever.
Some antihistamines can make you feel sleepy.

senses.definition: Relating to drugs that reduce the effects of histamine.
examples: The doctor prescribed an antihistamine cream for the rash.
Antihistamine drugs are commonly used in allergy season.

senses.definition: Drugs that reduce the effects of histamine and are used to treat allergies and some stomach problems.
examples: Antihistamines are often used to treat seasonal allergies.
Some antihistamines can cause drowsiness as a side effect.

senses.definition: Having the effect of reducing or blocking the action of histamine.
examples: The drug has strong antihistaminic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Antihistaminic activity was observed in animal tests.

senses.definition: A drug or substance that reduces or blocks the action of histamine.
examples: The new antihistaminic showed fewer side effects in trials.
Patients were given an antihistaminic before the procedure.

senses.definition: Drugs or substances that reduce or block the action of histamine.
examples: Modern antihistaminics usually cause less drowsiness.
The study compared different antihistaminics in treating asthma.

senses.definition: Ignoring, rejecting, or going against the facts or methods of history.
examples: He called the film’s version of events deeply antihistorical.
An antihistorical reading of the novel removes it from its real context.

senses.definition: Strongly disliking or opposed to holidays or celebrations.
examples: My uncle is so antiholiday that he refuses to decorate the house.
Her antiholiday mood made the party feel awkward.

senses.definition: Opposed to or discriminating against homosexual people or homosexuality.
examples: The law has been criticized as clearly antihomosexual.
Antihomosexual attitudes can cause serious harm to LGBTQ communities.

senses.definition: A substance that blocks or opposes the action of a hormone.
examples: Doctors may use an antihormone to slow the growth of the tumor.
Antihormones are important in the treatment of some cancers.

senses.definition: Substances that block or oppose the action of hormones.
examples: Antihormones are used in various treatments for breast cancer.
Researchers are testing new antihormones for endocrine disorders.

senses.definition: Opposing or calming displays of anger or offended pride.
examples: He tried an antihuff approach, joking until everyone relaxed.
Her antihuff comments were meant to defuse the situation.

senses.definition: Designed to reduce or remove unwanted humming or buzzing noise, especially in audio equipment.
examples: The engineer added an antihum filter to clean up the recording.
Antihum circuits can remove the low buzzing heard in cheap speakers.

senses.definition: Hostile to human beings or to human values such as kindness and dignity.
examples: They condemned the policy as cruel and antihuman.
Antihuman attitudes can lead to serious abuses of power.

senses.definition: A philosophy or attitude that criticizes or rejects humanism and its focus on human value and reason.
examples: Some postmodern thinkers have been accused of antihumanism.
The essay explores antihumanism in twentieth-century theory.

senses.definition: Different forms or versions of antihumanism.
examples: The article compares various antihumanisms in modern theory.
These antihumanisms challenge the idea that humans are at the center of everything.

senses.definition: A person who criticizes or rejects humanism and its focus on human value and reason.
examples: He is often described as an antihumanist in his philosophical writings.
Antihumanists argue that humanism hides structures of power.

senses.definition: Relating to or showing the ideas of antihumanism.
examples: Her antihumanist stance rejects the idea of a stable human subject.
The book gives an antihumanist reading of classic texts.

senses.definition: Opposed to the ideas or values of humanism, especially in philosophy or art.
examples: The essay offers an antihumanistic critique of modern culture.
Some theories take an antihumanistic view of human freedom.
